problem.title: First Missing Positive
problem.description: Given an unsorted integer array nums, return the smallest missing positive integer. You must implement an algorithm that runs in O(n) time and uses O(1) auxiliary space.
assumptions: Array may contain duplicates and negative numbers.
Must run in O(n) time and O(1) space.
Answer is always in range [1, n+1].

example.explanation: 1 is present, 2 is missing. The answer is always in [1, n+1]. Place each number x in the range [1,n] at index x-1, then scan for the first index i where nums[i] != i+1.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Index as Hash (Cyclic Sort) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use the array itself as a hash map. For every number x in [1, n], swap it to index x-1. After rearranging, scan left to right: the first position i where nums[i] != i+1 means i+1 is the missing positive. The key insight is that the answer must be in [1, n+1], so we only care about numbers in that range and can use indices directly.
